hypothesis: Pivot point breakouts capture genuine directional moves, ADX > 25 confirms a strong trend and filters out weak or false breakouts, and exiting when price re‑enters the Donchian channel captures the reversal; ATR‑scaled stop‑loss and take‑profit adapt to market volatility, improving risk‑adjusted performance.

Pivot point breakouts capture short‑term supply/demand shifts; filtering them with ADX ensures the market is trending enough to sustain the move, reducing false breakouts in choppy periods, while ATR‑based stop‑loss and take‑profit size risk proportionally to recent volatility, limiting drawdowns.

Combining a pivot point breakout with a volume oscillator confirms genuine buying or selling pressure, while an ADX filter ensures the market is trending enough to sustain the move, reducing false breakouts that caused large drawdowns; ATR‑based stops and targets then size risk consistently.

Breakouts above the pivot resistance or below the pivot support tend to generate strong moves; filtering those breakouts with ADX > 25 ensures the market is trending, which reduces false breakouts in sideways periods, while ATR‑based stop‑loss and profit targets scale the risk to current volatility, providing a consistent risk‑reward profile.

Breakouts through the pivot point resistance or support levels indicate strong directional moves; filtering these breakouts with ADX > 25 ensures the market is trending, while requiring ATR above a minimum filters out low‑volatility false breakouts, resulting in higher win rates and more reliable profit targets sized to volatility.

A breakout above the pivot resistance (R1) or below the pivot support (S1) is more reliable when the market shows a strong trend (ADX > 25) and sufficient volatility (ATR above a minimal threshold); this filters out noise and sideways moves, while using ATR‑based stops and TP scales risk proportionally to current volatility, improving win‑rate and limiting drawdowns.

A fast EMA identifies the current price direction, while ADX confirms a strong trend, so entries occur only when price is aligned with a confirmed trend; ATR‑based stop‑loss and profit targets scale risk to recent volatility, reducing false breakouts on the 1‑hour crypto chart.

Adding a strong ADX trend filter and an ATR volatility filter to the pivot point breakout rule should suppress false breakouts that occur in low‑volatility, non‑trending periods, thereby improving win rate and Sharpe while preserving the original edge of capturing genuine breakout moves.
